title: Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view
design_brief: A custom scrollable view (canvas, map) accepting two-finger pan scroll, like iPad trackpad scrolling. Reference: Procreate canvas, Figma mobile.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single-finger scroll on canvas (conflicts with draw)
mobile_reasoning: Gesture.Pan().minPointers(2).maxPointers(2). On end, withDecay for momentum.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Decay on release.
gesture_strategy: Two-finger Pan + simultaneous draw pen.
accessibility_considerations: Provide alternative scroll buttons for VoiceOver.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Multi-touch only. 2) Native decay physics. 3) Composes with single-finger drawing.

title: Modal stack with dismissable cards (Apple Wallet style)
design_brief: Multiple card-style modals stacked, swipe down to dismiss top one revealing next. Reference: Apple Wallet passes.
mobile_reasoning: Each card has progressively higher Y-offset. Top card's pan dismisses it.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const WSTACK = {offsetPerCard: 40};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Per-card spring.
gesture_strategy: Pan on top.
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Translation and backdrop opacity are derived on the UI thread with one shared value.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Apple Wallet pass dismissal. 2) Stack metaphor.

title: FlashList with separators that don't break recycling
design_brief: List with separators between items. Implemented via ItemSeparatorComponent (preserves recycling).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Separator as part of each item (breaks size estimation)
mobile_reasoning: FlashList's ItemSeparatorComponent renders between items without affecting cell size.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Separator outside cell — no size variance.

title: Skeleton-to-content fade swap
design_brief: When data loads, skeleton fades out and real content fades in at the same time (crossfade). Reference: any well-designed loading state.
mobile_reasoning: Render both with absolute positioning + opacity transition. Skeleton opacity 1→0; content 0→1.

title: Live transcription overlay for video
design_brief: Video player with live captions overlay (TikTok-style auto captions). Reference: TikTok, Instagram Reels.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No styling for legibility (use background pill)
mobile_reasoning: Captions positioned at bottom third. Background pill with auto-shrink to text width.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Caption swap fades.
gesture_strategy: Tap to pick or capture media; long running upload work is represented as cancellable queue state instead of blocking gestures.
accessibility_considerations: Picker, queue status, and failed upload states are announced with labels and live status text.
performance_notes: Uploads stream progress per item and keep large media out of React state except URI/status metadata.

title: Geofence visualization on map
design_brief: Display a circular geofence radius on map. User can drag center or resize radius. Reference: Reminders 'Remind me here', Tile geofences.
mobile_reasoning: react-native-maps Circle component. Center marker draggable. Radius slider OR pinch-to-resize.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Camera animation is short and deterministic; selection sheet appears without re-laying out the map.
gesture_strategy: Marker drag.
accessibility_considerations: Markers have titles and descriptions, and the selected place is repeated in a readable detail sheet.
performance_notes: Map camera changes are imperative through refs; marker state is small and does not rerender tiles.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native draggable marker. 2) Map-native circle overlay.

title: App icon: alternate icons (iOS) and adaptive (Android)

anti_patterns_to_avoid: Same icon both platforms (Android needs adaptive)
mobile_reasoning: iOS: setAlternateIconName from expo-system-ui or react-native-change-icon. Android: configure adaptiveIcon in app.json.

accessibility_considerations: Picker, queue status, and failed upload states are announced with labels and live status text.
performance_notes: Uploads stream progress per item and keep large media out of React state except URI/status metadata.
what_makes_this_native: Uses native platform primitives rather than web-only assumptions, so touch, keyboard, safe areas, and accessibility stay coherent.

title: Y2K aesthetic: chrome, bevels, vibrant hyperlink-blue
design_brief: A nostalgic Y2K-era app skin: chrome bevels, beveled buttons, hyperlink blue, sans-serif. Reference: 2000s web design / Geocities revival.
mobile_reasoning: Layered shadows for chrome effect. Bright #0000EE blue.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const Y2K = {blue:'#0000EE', chrome:'#C0C0C0'};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Mimics OS-level controls of yore. 2) Tactile via shadows.

title: Memory profiling with Flipper / DevTools
design_brief: Pattern for finding memory leaks in RN apps. Common leaks: forgotten subscriptions, ref accumulation, lazy imports never released.
mobile_reasoning: Profile via Hermes profiler + Flipper. Common pattern: every useEffect with subscription needs cleanup.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Expose role, state, and labels for the semantic action; add non-gesture alternatives for keyboard/switch users.
performance_notes: Profiled via Flipper or Hermes profiler.
